| MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | # Create React App Firebase Starter Template | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/cra-template-firebase-starter.svg)](https://npmjs.com/package/cra-template-firebase-starter) | MCP:BrQZKa | [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/dy/cra-template-firebase-starter.svg)](https://npmjs.com/package/cra-template-firebase-starter) | MCP:BrQZKa | [![License MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-orange.svg?style=flat)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pettiboy/cra-template-firebase-starter/main/LICENSE) | MCP:BrQZKa | [![PRs Welcome](https://img.shields.io/badge/PRs-Welcome-brightgreen.svg)](https://github.com/pettiboy/cra-template-firebase-starter/pulls) | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-20232A?style=for-the-badge&logo=react&logoColor=61DAFB) | MCP:BrQZKa | ![NPM](https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-CB3837?style=for-the-badge&logo=npm&logoColor=white) | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | Make sure you ⭐️ this [`repository`](https://github.com/pettiboy/cra-template-firebase-starter) if you find it helpful or interesting :) | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa |
| MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ## ✨ Installation | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ```bash | MCP:BrQZKa | npx create-react-app your-project-name --template firebase-starter | MCP:BrQZKa | ``` | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | OR | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ```bash | MCP:BrQZKa | yarn create react-app your-project-name --template firebase-starter | MCP:BrQZKa | ``` | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | `NOTE:` This `template` is also available in `TypeScript` [here](https://github.com/pettiboy/cra-template-typescript-firebase). | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ## ⚙️ Usage | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ### SetUp firebase | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 1. Go to your [`Firebase Console`](https://console.firebase.google.com/) | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 2. Click on `Add project`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | - Follow steps and create project. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 3. Click on web icon. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | - Register web app. | MCP:BrQZKa | - You will get details for setting up `Firebase SDK`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 4. Add the config details in your `.env` file (available in root of your project). | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ### SetUp SignIn with google | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 1. In your project console, click on `Authentication` and `Get Started`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 2. In `Sign-in method`, click on `Google`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 3. Toggle `Enable` and click on `Save`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ### To create a new screen | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 1. Create a new component in `src/screens`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 2. In your `src/config/routes.ts`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ```js | MCP:BrQZKa | { | MCP:BrQZKa | path: "/your-route", | MCP:BrQZKa | component: YourScreenComponentName, | MCP:BrQZKa | name: "Screen Name For Reference", | MCP:BrQZKa | protected: false, // if user needs to be authenticated to access this screen | MCP:BrQZKa | } | MCP:BrQZKa | ``` | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ### To Deploy on GitHub Pages | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 1. Add your domain (your-github-username.github.io) to the OAuth redirect domains list in the `Firebase console -> Auth section -> Sign in method tab`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 2. update the `homepage` field in your `package.json` to the domain you are hosting on. (By default it is: `http://your-github-username.github.io/your-repo-name`) | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 3. (Optional) Test production build locally: Update the `homepage` field to `/` and run: | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ```bash | MCP:BrQZKa | npm i -g serve # install serve globally via npm | MCP:BrQZKa | npm run build-and-serve | MCP:BrQZKa | ``` | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | 4. Deploy to GitHub Pages run: | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ```bash | MCP:BrQZKa | npm run deploy | MCP:BrQZKa | ``` | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ## ✨ Featues | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ### 1. Routing | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | Routing setup using `react-router-dom`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | - With easily implementable private routes (routes only authenticated users can use). | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | - Easily add new screens. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ### 2. Authentication | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | Authentication implemented implemented | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | SignIn with Google implemented using `firebase` and `react-firebase-hooks`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ### 3. Firebase Configured | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | Firebase configured with `GoogleAuthProvider` implemented. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ### 4. Material UI | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | Login Screen UI built using `@mui/material`. | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ### 5. Deploy on Github Pages | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | Deploying on GitHub pages powered by `gh-pages` and [`spa-github-pages`](https://github.com/rafgraph/spa-github-pages) | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ## 🔧 File Structure | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ``` | MCP:BrQZKa | ├── src | MCP:BrQZKa | │ ├── components | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ ├── auth | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ | ├── AuthChecker.js | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ | ├── AuthContainer.js | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ | ├── Logout.js | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ ├── utils | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ | ├── Center.js | MCP:BrQZKa | │ ├── config | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ ├── firebase.config.js | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ ├── firebase.js | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ ├── routes.js | MCP:BrQZKa | │ ├── screens | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ ├── Login.js | MCP:BrQZKa | │ │ ├── Home.js | MCP:BrQZKa | ``` | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | ## 📝 Authors | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | - Hussain Pettiwala ([@pettiboy](https://github.com/pettiboy)) | MCP:BrQZKa | | MCP:BrQZKa | Special thanks to this well written [`blog`](https://grischuk.de/how-to-create-custom-create-react-app-cra-templates) and this [`repo`](https://github.com/alexandr-g/cra-template-typescript-redux) by [alexandr-g](https://github.com/alexandr-g).

data.content:
Here's my findings:

---

## NPM Scripts Summary

### Root `package.json` (template developer scripts)

| Script | Command | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `start` | `react-scripts start` | Local dev server (hot-reload) |
| `build` | `react-scripts build` | Create a production build |
| `test` | `react-scripts test` | Run tests |
| `eject` | `react-scripts eject` | Eject from Create React App |
| `predeploy` | `npm run build` | Automatically runs build before deploy |
| `deploy` | `gh-pages -d build` | Publish build to GitHub Pages |
| `clean-files` | `rm -rf ./template/public ./template/src` | Purge old template source files |
| `copy-files` | `cp -a ./src/. template/src && cp -a ./public/. template/public` | Copy fresh source into template directory |
| `prepublishOnly` | `npm run clean-files && npm run copy-files` | Automatically syncs source into template before `npm publish` |

### `template.json` (scripts users receive after scaffolding from this template)

| Script | Command | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `start` | `react-scripts start` | Local dev server |
| `build` | `react-scripts build` | Production build |
| `test` | `react-scripts test` | Run tests |
| `eject` | `react-scripts eject` | Eject from CRA |
| **`serve`** | `serve -s build` | Serve the production build locally as a static SPA |
| **`build-and-serve`** | `npm run build && npm run serve` | Build + serve production build locally |
| `predeploy` | `npm run build` | Auto-build before deploy |
| `deploy` | `gh-pages -d build` | Deploy to GitHub Pages |
| `pull` | `git pull && npm i && npm start` | Pull latest, install deps, and start dev server |

---

## Guidance for Testing a Production Build Locally Before Publishing

The README (under **"To Deploy on GitHub Pages"**, step 3) spells this out:

1. **Install the `serve` package globally** (one-time):
   ```bash
   npm i -g serve
   ```
2. **(Optional)** Set the `homepage` field in your `package.json` to `"/"` (so asset paths resolve correctly for local serving rather than a GitHub Pages sub-path).
3. **Build and serve the production bundle locally:**
   ```bash
   npm run build-and-serve
   ```
   This runs `npm run build` (producing an optimized production bundle in `/build`) and then `npm run serve` (which launches `serve -s build` — a static file server that treats the app as a single-page application).

The template deliberately ships the `serve` and `build-and-serve` scripts so end users can verify the production build behaves correctly on their machine before pushing to GitHub Pages via `npm run deploy`.
